---
title: "Predict Percent Body Fat"
author: "David A Hughes"
date: "2026-02-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
kids2 <- kids %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("f", "m")),
    log_height = log(height),
    log_weight = log(weight)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(percent_fat), !is.na(log_height), !is.na(log_weight), !is.na(age), !is.na(sex))

```


```{r}
library(mgcv)
library(tidyverse)

####################################
## 10-fold CV for GAM
####################################
set.seed(123)
n         = nrow(kids2)
fold_ids  = sample(rep(1:10, length.out = n))

cv_results = vector("list", 10)

for (k in 1:10) {
  val_idx   = which(fold_ids == k)
  train_idx = which(fold_ids != k)
  
  ## fit on training folds
  fold_fit = gam(
    percent_fat ~ sex + 
                  s(age, by = sex, k = 12) + 
                  te(log_height, log_weight, by = sex, k = c(10, 10)),
    data   = kids2[train_idx, ],
    method = "REML"
  )
  
  ## predict on held-out fold
  preds = predict(fold_fit, newdata = kids2[val_idx, ], type = "response")
  obs   = kids2$percent_fat[val_idx]
  sex   = kids2$sex[val_idx]
  age   = kids2$age[val_idx]
  
  cv_results[[k]] = tibble(
    fold    = k,
    obs     = obs,
    pred    = preds,
    sex     = sex,
    age     = age,
    val_idx = val_idx
  )
}

cv_df = bind_rows(cv_results)

####################################
## Overall CV metrics
####################################
cv_metrics = cv_df |>
  summarise(
    R2   = cor(obs, pred)^2,
    MAE  = mean(abs(obs - pred)),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((obs - pred)^2))
  )

print(cv_metrics)

####################################
## CV metrics stratified by sex
####################################
cv_metrics_by_sex = cv_df |>
  group_by(sex) |>
  summarise(
    R2   = cor(obs, pred)^2,
    MAE  = mean(abs(obs - pred)),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((obs - pred)^2)),
    n    = n()
  )

print(cv_metrics_by_sex)

####################################
## CV metrics stratified by sex and age
####################################
cv_metrics_by_age_sex = cv_df |>
  group_by(sex, age) |>
  summarise(
    R2   = cor(obs, pred)^2,
    MAE  = mean(abs(obs - pred)),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((obs - pred)^2)),
    n    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

####################################
## Add CV predictions back to kids2
####################################
kids2 = kids2 |>
  mutate(
    pbf_cv_pred  = cv_df$pred[order(cv_df$val_idx)],
    pbf_cv_resid = percent_fat - pbf_cv_pred
  )


```

## Plots

```{r, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 10}
####################################
## Plots
####################################

## 1. Overall observed vs predicted (CV)
p1 = cv_df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = obs, y = pred, color = sex)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x), se = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x        = "observed % body fat",
       y        = "predicted % body fat",
       title    = "10-fold CV: observed vs predicted",
       subtitle = paste0("R²: ",   round(cv_metrics$R2,   3),
                         "  |  MAE: ",  round(cv_metrics$MAE,  2), "%",
                         "  |  RMSE: ", round(cv_metrics$RMSE, 2), "%"))

## 2. Residuals by age and sex (CV)
p2 = cv_df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = obs - pred, color = sex)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x), se = TRUE) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x     = "age (years)",
       y     = "residual (obs - pred)",
       title = "CV residuals by age and sex")

## 3. MAE by age and sex
p3 = cv_metrics_by_age_sex |>
  ggplot(aes(x = age, y = MAE, color = sex)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n)) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x     = "age (years)",
       y     = "MAE (% body fat)",
       title = "CV MAE by age and sex",
       size  = "n")

p1 / (p2 | p3)

```

## Final Model

```{r}
####################################
## Final model (fit on all data)
## This is the model to deploy
####################################
fit_pbf_final = gam(
  percent_fat ~ sex + 
                s(age, by = sex, k = 12) + 
                te(log_height, log_weight, by = sex, k = c(10, 10)),
  data   = kids2,
  method = "REML"
)

## save for use in Shiny app
saveRDS(fit_pbf_final, "../../results/fit_pbf_final.rds")
```


## SHINY APP

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(mgcv)
library(tidyverse)

## load the saved model
fit_pbf_final = readRDS("fit_pbf_final.rds")

ui = fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Predicted % Body Fat in Children & Adolescents"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput("sex",     "Sex",         choices = c("Female" = "f", "Male" = "m")),
      numericInput("age",    "Age (years)", value = 10,  min = 2,   max = 18,  step = 1),
      numericInput("height", "Height (m)", value = 1.40, min = 0.50, max = 2.20, step = 0.01),
      numericInput("weight", "Weight (kg)", value = 35, min = 5, max = 200, step = 0.5),
      radioButtons("ci_level", "Prediction interval level",
             choices  = c("25%" = 0.25, "50%" = 0.50, "75%" = 0.75, "95%" = 0.95),
             selected = 0.95,
             inline   = TRUE),
      actionButton("predict_btn", "Predict", class = "btn-primary")
    ),
    mainPanel(
      h3("Predicted % Body Fat"),
      tableOutput("pred_table"),
      plotOutput("pred_plot", height = "300px"),
      br(),
      p(em("Prediction is based on a GAM fitted to NHANES data using sex, age,
            height, and weight. The prediction interval reflects uncertainty in
            where an individual observation is likely to fall, not just uncertainty
            in the mean. For reference purposes only."))
    )
  )
)

server = function(input, output, session) {
  
  result = eventReactive(input$predict_btn, {
    
    new_data = tibble(
      sex        = factor(input$sex, levels = c("f", "m")),
      age        = as.numeric(input$age),
      log_height = log(as.numeric(input$height)),   ## now metres, matches training
      log_weight = log(as.numeric(input$weight))
    )
    
    pred_obj = predict(fit_pbf_final, newdata = new_data,
                       type = "response", se.fit = TRUE)
    
    fit   = as.numeric(pred_obj$fit)
    se    = as.numeric(pred_obj$se.fit)
    sigma = sqrt(fit_pbf_final$sig2)
    se_pred = sqrt(se^2 + sigma^2)
    
    ## build one row per selected interval level
    levels_selected = as.numeric(input$ci_level)
    
    interval_df = purrr::map_dfr(levels_selected, function(lvl) {
      z = qnorm(1 - (1 - lvl) / 2)
      tibble(
        level    = lvl,
        fit      = round(fit, 1),
        ci_lower = round(fit - z * se,      1),
        ci_upper = round(fit + z * se,      1),
        pi_lower = round(fit - z * se_pred, 1),
        pi_upper = round(fit + z * se_pred, 1)
      )
    }) |> arrange(level)
    
    interval_df
  })
    
  output$pred_table = renderTable({
    r = result()
    
    df = tibble(
      predicted = paste0(r$fit,      "%"),
      ci_lower  = paste0(r$ci_lower, "%"),
      ci_upper  = paste0(r$ci_upper, "%"),
      pi_lower  = paste0(r$pi_lower, "%"),
      pi_upper  = paste0(r$pi_upper, "%")
    )
    
    names(df) = c(
      "Predicted % fat",
      paste0(round(r$ci * 100), "% CI lower"),
      paste0(round(r$ci * 100), "% CI upper"),
      paste0(round(r$ci * 100), "% PI lower"),
      paste0(round(r$ci * 100), "% PI upper")
    )
    
    df
  })
  
  output$pred_plot = renderPlot({
    r = result()
    
    out_of_range = any(r$fit < 0 | r$fit > 60)
    fit_val      = r$fit[1]   ## same for all rows
    
    ## one row per interval level, CI and PI overlaid on the same row
    p = ggplot() +
      ## PI rectangle (wider, lighter)
      geom_rect(data = r,
                aes(xmin = pmax(pi_lower, 0), xmax = pmin(pi_upper, 70),
                    ymin = as.numeric(factor(level)) - 0.3,
                    ymax = as.numeric(factor(level)) + 0.3,
                    fill = "Prediction Interval"),
                alpha = 0.15) +
      ## CI rectangle (narrower, darker, overlaid)
      geom_rect(data = r,
                aes(xmin = pmax(ci_lower, 0), xmax = pmin(ci_upper, 70),
                    ymin = as.numeric(factor(level)) - 0.18,
                    ymax = as.numeric(factor(level)) + 0.18,
                    fill = "Confidence Interval"),
                alpha = 0.35) +
      ## PI whisker line
      geom_segment(data = r,
                   aes(x = pmax(pi_lower, 0), xend = pmin(pi_upper, 70),
                       y = as.numeric(factor(level)),
                       yend = as.numeric(factor(level)),
                       color = "Prediction Interval"),
                   linewidth = 1) +
      ## CI whisker line
      geom_segment(data = r,
                   aes(x = pmax(ci_lower, 0), xend = pmin(ci_upper, 70),
                       y = as.numeric(factor(level)),
                       yend = as.numeric(factor(level)),
                       color = "Confidence Interval"),
                   linewidth = 1.5) +
      ## point estimate dot
      geom_point(data = r,
                 aes(x = fit, y = as.numeric(factor(level))),
                 color = "black", size = 4, zorder = 5) +
      ## point estimate label (top row only, to avoid repetition)
      geom_text(data = r |> filter(level == max(level)),
                aes(x = fit, y = as.numeric(factor(level)) + 0.45,
                    label = paste0("Predicted: ", fit, "%")),
                size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
      ## PI lower bound labels
      geom_text(data = r,
                aes(x = pmax(pi_lower, 0),
                    y = as.numeric(factor(level)) - 0.42,
                    label = paste0(pi_lower, "%"),
                    color = "Prediction Interval"),
                size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
      ## PI upper bound labels
      geom_text(data = r,
                aes(x = pmin(pi_upper, 70),
                    y = as.numeric(factor(level)) - 0.42,
                    label = paste0(pi_upper, "%"),
                    color = "Prediction Interval"),
                size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
      ## CI lower bound labels
      # geom_text(data = r,
      #           aes(x = pmax(ci_lower, 0),
      #               y = as.numeric(factor(level)) + 0.42,
      #               label = paste0(ci_lower, "%"),
      #               color = "Confidence Interval"),
      #           size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
      # ## CI upper bound labels
      # geom_text(data = r,
      #           aes(x = pmin(ci_upper, 70),
      #               y = as.numeric(factor(level)) + 0.42,
      #               label = paste0(ci_upper, "%"),
      #               color = "Confidence Interval"),
      #           size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 70),
                         labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
      scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq_along(unique(r$level)),
                         labels = paste0(sort(unique(r$level)) * 100, "%")) +
      scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Prediction Interval" = "steelblue",
                                    "Confidence Interval" = "tomato")) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("Prediction Interval" = "steelblue",
                                    "Confidence Interval" = "tomato")) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position    = "bottom",
            legend.title       = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y       = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()) +
      labs(x     = "% body fat",
           title = "Confidence and prediction intervals by level")
    
    if (out_of_range) {
      p = p +
        annotate("text", x = 35, y = max(as.numeric(factor(r$level))) + 0.7,
                 label = "⚠️ prediction may be unreliable — inputs outside training range",
                 color = "darkorange", size = 4, fontface = "bold")
    }
    
    p
  })
  
}


shinyApp(ui, server)
```
